title: The Agency Efficiency Score
subtitle: How Much Profit Are You Leaving on the Table?

persona_selector.text: What size is your agency right now?

answers.label: Boutique team of 10-20 people
Mid-sized shop of 21-40 people
Established agency of 41-75 people
Solo founder or under 10, planning to scale

questions.text: How are inbound leads currently qualified before they hit a sales call?

answers.label: A team member manually reviews and calls every lead
We use a basic intake form, then someone reviews manually
Form plus some scoring rules, but humans still triage most
Automated scoring routes most leads, humans handle edge cases
Fully automated qualification with AI scoring and routing

questions.text: How long does it take from a lead submitting a form to getting a response?

answers.label: More than 24 hours, sometimes days
Usually within a business day
Within 2-4 hours during business hours
Under 30 minutes most of the time
Instant automated reply with personalized next steps

questions.text: How is your sales pipeline data tracked and updated?

answers.label: Spreadsheets and memory
CRM exists but updates are inconsistent
CRM is updated manually after each touchpoint
CRM auto-syncs with calendar, email, and forms
Fully automated pipeline with AI summaries and next-step suggestions

questions.text: How are discovery call notes captured and turned into proposals?

answers.label: Handwritten or memory, proposal built from scratch each time
Typed notes, proposal cobbled together from old templates
Recorded calls with manual transcripts, template-based proposals
AI transcribes and summarizes, proposal partially auto-generated
AI generates a custom proposal draft from the call automatically

questions.text: How much of your founder or sales lead's week is spent on unqualified leads?

answers.label: More than 15 hours, it eats most of the week
8-15 hours weekly
4-8 hours weekly
1-4 hours weekly
Almost zero, only qualified leads reach them

low.headline: Your sales pipeline is leaking time and revenue
low.text: Right now your team is doing the work of a basic automation system. Every hour spent qualifying cold leads is an hour not spent closing warm ones. The fix is sequencing, not more headcount.
steps: Add an intake form with 5-7 qualifying questions and route submissions into a CRM automatically
Set up an instant auto-reply with calendar booking for leads above a scoring threshold
Implement an AI call summarizer that drafts the first version of every proposal from discovery notes

mid.headline: You have the foundations, now tighten the loop
mid.text: Your pipeline works but still depends on humans for the connective tissue between stages. That is where time leaks. Closing those gaps usually frees up 10-15 hours a week for your sales lead.
steps: Auto-score leads on intake and route only the top tier to your sales team
Connect your CRM to email, calendar, and form tools so updates happen without manual entry
Use AI to generate proposal first drafts directly from call transcripts within 24 hours

high.headline: Your pipeline is already a competitive edge
high.text: You are running ahead of most agencies your size. The play now is depth, not patching gaps. Use the time you have freed up to invest in offer design, retention, and higher-leverage outbound.
steps: Layer in predictive lead scoring using historical close-rate data
Add AI-driven account research that enriches every qualified lead with context
Build a feedback loop that improves your scoring model from won and lost deals each quarter

questions.text: How long does it take from contract signed to work officially starting?

answers.label: More than 3 weeks, often longer
2-3 weeks
1-2 weeks
3-7 days
Under 72 hours

questions.text: How are onboarding documents and asset requests sent to new clients?

answers.label: Custom email written from scratch each time
Email template that someone manually personalizes
Standard onboarding packet sent manually after signing
Triggered automatically after contract signing, with reminders
Fully automated onboarding portal with progress tracking

questions.text: How is the brand audit or strategy doc created for each new client?

answers.label: Built from scratch by a strategist over several days
Strategist uses old docs as inspiration, builds manually
Template exists but most sections are still hand-written
AI drafts most sections from intake data, strategist refines
AI generates a complete first draft within hours of onboarding

questions.text: How are project management tools and folders set up for new clients?

answers.label: PM creates everything manually each time
Copy-paste from a previous client, then edit
Template project that gets duplicated and renamed
One-click or trigger-based project setup with all assets
Fully automated provisioning the moment a contract is signed

questions.text: How often do clients complain or churn early because onboarding felt slow or messy?

answers.label: It happens regularly, we have lost clients in the first 60 days
Occasionally, usually one or two per quarter
Rarely, but feedback mentions it
Almost never, onboarding is a strength
Clients consistently praise our onboarding experience

low.headline: Onboarding is silently costing you clients
low.text: The first 30 days set the tone for the entire engagement. Slow, manual onboarding does not just waste your team's hours, it shakes client confidence before you have done any real work. This is one of the highest-ROI areas to fix first.
steps: Build a standardized onboarding workflow that triggers the moment a contract is signed
Create a templated brand audit that AI can populate from intake form responses
Set up automated PM project provisioning so no setup work is done by hand

mid.headline: Solid onboarding, but inconsistent execution
mid.text: Your team has the right pieces, they just are not chained together. The handoffs between sales, ops, and delivery are where time and quality slip. Tightening this saves days per client and protects margin.
steps: Connect your contract signing tool to automatically kick off onboarding tasks
Use AI to draft the strategy and brand audit docs from existing client intake data
Add a client-facing portal that shows onboarding progress and required actions

high.headline: Onboarding is a real differentiator for you
high.text: Most agencies cannot say this. You are starting engagements with clear momentum. Now use that as a sales asset and continue to refine the experience for retention.
steps: Showcase your onboarding process in sales conversations as proof of operational maturity
Add post-onboarding NPS surveys and feed responses back into refinement cycles
Identify the one remaining manual step and automate it this quarter

questions.text: How much of your team's content production is fully manual?

answers.label: Nearly all of it, every asset is built from scratch
Most of it, with some templated sections
About half manual, half templated or AI-assisted
Mostly AI-assisted with human polish
Fully systemized production with AI doing first drafts and humans approving

questions.text: How are content briefs created for each client deliverable?

answers.label: Written from scratch by a strategist or PM
Brief template filled out manually each time
Template plus reference docs, partially copy-pasted
AI generates the brief from client strategy and past assets
Briefs auto-generate from project data with full client context

questions.text: How is creative quality reviewed and approved internally?

answers.label: Founder or senior reviews everything personally
Senior reviews most things, with email back-and-forth
Defined review checklist, but execution varies
Structured QA workflow with assigned roles and clear criteria
Automated QA pre-checks plus a human approval step

questions.text: How long does an average deliverable spend in revisions or rework?

answers.label: More than 5 rounds is common
3-5 rounds typical
2-3 rounds typical
Usually 1-2 rounds
Most deliverables are approved on the first or second pass

questions.text: How is repeat work like reporting, recap decks, or social variations handled?

answers.label: Built from scratch each time
Templates exist but require heavy manual updates
Templates plus copy-paste of recurring data
Mostly auto-generated from data sources with minor edits
Fully automated reports and variations delivered on schedule

low.headline: Your team is rebuilding the wheel every week
low.text: Production is where agency margin lives or dies. If your team is doing manual work that could be templated or AI-assisted, you are paying senior salaries for junior tasks. Fix this and you free up your most expensive hours.
steps: Audit the last 10 deliverables and identify the 3 most repeated tasks to systemize first
Build AI-assisted brief generation from client strategy docs and past assets
Templatize recurring deliverables like reports and recap decks with auto-populated data

mid.headline: You have systems, now scale them
mid.text: Production is no longer chaos but it still consumes more senior time than it should. The next move is shifting first drafts to AI and reserving humans for judgment, polish, and client-facing strategy.
steps: Move first drafts of routine content to AI with structured human QA
Define a clear review workflow with role-specific checklists
Automate any deliverable that uses the same data inputs every cycle

high.headline: Production is a profit engine, not a cost center
high.text: You have built the rare agency where output scales without proportional headcount. The risk now is complacency. Keep finding the slowest 10 percent of your process and improving it.
steps: Run a quarterly production audit to find the slowest workflows
Invest in custom AI agents trained on your house style and client voices
Use saved capacity to launch a productized service with predictable margin

questions.text: How are weekly or monthly client status updates created?

answers.label: Account manager writes each one from scratch
Email template that gets manually filled in each time
Template plus pulled data, manually assembled
Auto-generated draft pulled from PM and analytics tools
Fully automated client updates sent on schedule with AI summaries

questions.text: How are performance reports built for clients?

answers.label: Built manually in slides each reporting cycle
Template deck with manually pasted data
Template plus dashboard screenshots
Auto-generated reports with AI-written commentary
Live dashboards plus auto-generated executive summaries

questions.text: How are client questions and requests captured and routed?

answers.label: Email and Slack DMs, no central tracking
Some go to PM tool, others get lost in inboxes
Standard intake channel, manually triaged
Centralized intake routed automatically by topic
AI categorizes and routes every request with SLA tracking

questions.text: How quickly does the team respond to a client message?

answers.label: Often more than 24 hours
Within one business day, but inconsistent
Within 4-8 hours reliably
Within 1-2 hours during business time
Acknowledged instantly with AI, resolved within hours

questions.text: How is internal context shared across the account team?

answers.label: It mostly lives in one person's head
Scattered across email, Slack, and notes
Documented in PM tool but inconsistent
Centralized client knowledge base, kept current
AI-searchable knowledge base with full account history

low.headline: Your client experience depends on heroics
low.text: When communication relies on individual account managers remembering things and writing from scratch, every vacation, sick day, or staff change creates risk. Clients feel inconsistency even when they cannot name it.
steps: Centralize all client communication into one tool with clear ownership
Build a templated weekly update auto-populated from PM and analytics data
Create a shared client knowledge base that any team member can reference

mid.headline: Communication works, but it is fragile
mid.text: You have systems, but they still depend on humans showing up consistently. Automating the routine pieces lets your team spend their attention on strategic conversations rather than status updates.
steps: Automate weekly status emails using PM and analytics data
Add AI-generated executive summaries to performance reports
Implement a centralized request intake with auto-routing by topic

high.headline: Clients feel the difference, even if they cannot name it
high.text: Your communication systems are quietly elite. The opportunity now is using that strength to expand accounts and earn referrals from clients who cannot believe how organized you are.
steps: Use response time and reporting quality as proof points in renewal conversations
Layer AI-driven account insights into QBRs and strategy reviews
Train every new hire on the system before they touch a client account

questions.text: How is time tracking handled across the team?

answers.label: It is not, or only at month-end from memory
Tracked manually in spreadsheets
Time tracking tool exists but adoption is patchy
Automated tracking integrated with PM tool
Fully automated with AI categorization and profitability reporting

questions.text: How are invoices created and sent each month?

answers.label: Manually built in a spreadsheet or doc
Accounting tool, but invoices created manually
Recurring invoices set up but require manual review
Auto-generated from project data with one-click send
Fully automated billing tied to project milestones

questions.text: How visible is project profitability across active accounts?

answers.label: We rarely know which accounts are profitable
We calculate it once or twice a year
Quarterly profitability reviews, manually built
Monthly profitability dashboards per account
Real-time profitability visibility per account and team member

questions.text: How are recurring admin tasks like contracts, NDAs, and renewals handled?

answers.label: Founder or admin handles each one manually
Templates exist but are filled out by hand
Templates plus contract automation tool
Triggered automatically from CRM or PM events
Fully automated lifecycle from quote to renewal

questions.text: How much of your operations team's time is spent on repeatable admin work?

answers.label: More than 60 percent of their week
40-60 percent
20-40 percent
10-20 percent
Under 10 percent, they focus on improvement work

low.headline: You cannot manage what you cannot see
low.text: Without time, profitability, and admin systems in place, you are running your agency on intuition. Some accounts are subsidizing others, and you do not know which. This is the foundation everything else sits on.
steps: Implement automated time tracking integrated with your PM tool this month
Build a profitability dashboard per active account using real time and revenue data
Automate contracts, invoices, and renewals through a single connected stack

mid.headline: Operations are functional but underleveraged
mid.text: You have visibility, but not the kind that drives decisions weekly. The next step is moving from monthly reporting to real-time signals so problems are caught before they cost a quarter of margin.
steps: Move profitability reporting to a live dashboard refreshed daily
Auto-generate invoices from project milestones and tracked time
Identify the top 3 admin tasks consuming the most time and automate them

high.headline: Your back office runs like a SaaS business
high.text: Few agencies operate at this level of operational maturity. You can scale revenue without scaling overhead at the same rate. Now use that financial clarity to make sharper portfolio decisions.
steps: Use real-time profitability data to fire or restructure the bottom 10 percent of accounts
Build a quarterly ops review focused on automating one more layer of admin
Reinvest saved time into productizing services and building IP

cta.headline: Ready to stop bleeding profit on manual work?
cta.description: You just saw exactly where your agency is losing time and money. The next step is mapping a clear plan to fix the highest-impact gaps first. Book a 30-minute strategy call and I will walk you through the specific automations that match your score and your stack.
